title: Le français en route vers le DELF

days.title: La routine quotidienne
days.objective: Learn the key reflexive and everyday verbs used to describe your daily routine.
days.paretoFocus: A handful of routine verbs (se lever, se laver, manger, travailler) cover most A1 self-description tasks in the DELF oral.
drills: Say your morning routine aloud with three verbs
Conjugate se lever in je/tu/il
Write one sentence with 'd'abord' and 'ensuite'

items: Recall five daily-routine verbs from memory
Say 'I get up at 7' in French
Order the actions of a typical morning

activities.title: Les verbes de la routine / Daily routine verbs
activities.intro: These verbs let you describe what you do every day. Several are reflexive (with 'se'), so watch the pronoun.

items.hr: Je me lève à sept heures.
items.note: se lever is reflexive: je me lève

items.hr: Je me lave et je m'habille.
items.en: I wash and I get dressed.

items.note: prendre le petit-déjeuner = to have breakfast

items.hr: Le soir, je me couche tôt.
items.en: In the evening, I go to bed early.
items.note: se coucher = to go to bed

activities.title: Practice: routine verbs
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct form. Pay attention to reflexive pronouns.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I get up'?
questions.explanation: se lever = to get up; the reflexive form is 'je me lève'.

questions.prompt: Which verb means 'to go to bed'?
questions.explanation: se coucher = to go to bed.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I get dressed' (use je).
questions.explanation: s'habiller becomes 'je m'habille' with elision before the h.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I have breakfast'.
questions.explanation: prendre le petit-déjeuner = to have breakfast.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Je me lève à sept heures.'
questions.explanation: Subject + reflexive pronoun + verb + time expression.

activities.title: Ta routine du matin / Your morning routine
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and answer about your morning routine.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Bonjour ! Tu te lèves à quelle heure ?
lines.en: Hello! What time do you get up?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me lève à sept heures.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et après, qu'est-ce que tu fais ?
lines.en: And after, what do you do?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je me lave et je prends le petit-déjeuner.
lines.en: I wash and I have breakfast.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Do you work in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je travaille toute la journée.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: And do you go to bed early?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je me couche à onze heures.
lines.en: Yes, I go to bed at eleven.

days.title: Dire l'heure
days.objective: Learn to ask for and tell the time in French.
days.paretoFocus: Telling the time is tested directly in the DELF A1 and unlocks scheduling, appointments, and daily-routine talk.
drills: Read five clock times aloud in French
Ask 'What time is it?' two ways
Say a half past and a quarter past time

items: Recall 'et demie', 'et quart', 'moins le quart'
Say 3:30 and 8:45 in French
Ask the time politely

activities.title: Quelle heure est-il ? / What time is it?
activities.intro: Use 'il est ... heure(s)' to tell the time. Learn the words for half, quarter, and 'to'.

items.hr: Il est huit heures et quart.
Il est huit heures et demie.
Il est neuf heures moins le quart.

activities.title: Practice: telling the time
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct time expression.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'It's half past eight'?
options: Il est huit heures et demie.
Il est huit heures et quart.
Il est huit heures moins le quart.
questions.answer: Il est huit heures et demie.

questions.prompt: Which means 'It's noon'?
Type the French for 'It's a quarter to nine.'
questions.answer: Il est neuf heures moins le quart
questions.explanation: moins le quart = a quarter to, before the next hour.

questions.prompt: Type the French question 'What time is it?'
questions.explanation: Quelle heure est-il ? is the standard question.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Il est huit heures et quart.'
questions.explanation: Il est + hour + heures + et quart.

activities.title: Quelle heure est-il ? / Asking the time
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and tell the time when asked.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Excuse me, what time is it?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est neuf heures et quart.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Merci ! Le train part à quelle heure ?
lines.en: Thanks! What time does the train leave?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il part à neuf heures et demie.
lines.en: It leaves at half past nine.

days.title: Ma journée
days.objective: Combine routine verbs with times to describe a full day.
days.paretoFocus: Linking actions to times ('à midi je mange') is exactly the sentence pattern the DELF A1 oral rewards.
drills: Describe your day with three time markers
Use 'le matin', 'l'après-midi', 'le soir'
Say what you do at noon

items: Recall the parts of the day in French
Say 'At noon, I eat' in French
Chain two actions with 'puis'

activities.title: Décrire sa journée / Describing your day
activities.intro: Attach a time or a part of the day to each action to describe your whole day.

items.hr: Le matin, je vais au travail.
items.en: In the morning, I go to work.

items.hr: Le soir, je dîne à sept heures.
items.en: In the evening, I have dinner at seven.

activities.title: Practice: my day
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct expression to describe the day.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'At noon, I have lunch'?
questions.explanation: déjeuner = to have lunch; à midi = at noon.

questions.prompt: Which means 'in the afternoon'?
Type the French for 'In the evening, I have dinner.'
questions.explanation: dîner = to have dinner; le soir = in the evening.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I go to work' (use je).
questions.explanation: aller au travail = to go to work; je vais is the je form of aller.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Le soir, je dîne à sept heures.'
questions.explanation: Time of day + subject + verb + time.

activities.title: Raconte ta journée / Tell me about your day
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and describe your day.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu fais le matin ?
lines.en: What do you do in the morning?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le matin, je vais au travail.
lines.en: In the morning, I go to work.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: À midi, je déjeune avec mes collègues.
lines.en: At noon, I have lunch with my colleagues.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What do you do in the evening?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le soir, je dîne et je me repose.
lines.en: In the evening, I have dinner and I rest.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu as une journée bien remplie !

days.title: Les jours et les mois
days.objective: Learn the days of the week, months, and how to say dates.
days.paretoFocus: Days, months, and dates are high-frequency and needed for appointments and forms in the DELF A1.
drills: Recite the seven days in order
Recite the twelve months
Say today's date in French

items: Recall the days of the week
Say 'the 14th of July' in French
Ask 'What's the date today?'

activities.title: Les jours et les mois / Days and months
activities.intro: Days and months are not capitalised in French. Dates use 'le + number + month'.

items.hr: samedi et dimanche, c'est le week-end.
items.en: Saturday and Sunday, that's the weekend.

items.hr: janvier, février, mars, avril, mai, juin
Mon anniversaire est le premier mai.
items.en: My birthday is the first of May.

activities.title: Practice: days, months, dates
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct answer.

questions.prompt: Which day comes after 'mercredi'?
questions.explanation: The order is lundi, mardi, mercredi, jeudi...

questions.prompt: How do you say 'the 14th of July'?
questions.explanation: French dates are 'le + number + month', with no 'of'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'the first of May'.
questions.explanation: The 1st uses 'premier'; other dates use plain numbers.

questions.prompt: Type the French question 'What's the date today?' (use 'nous sommes').
questions.explanation: Quelle est la date aujourd'hui ? is a common way to ask.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Aujourd'hui, nous sommes le 14 juillet.'
questions.explanation: Nous sommes le + date is the standard structure.

activities.title: C'est quel jour ? / What day is it?
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and answer about days and dates.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Mon anniversaire est le premier mai.
lines.en: My birthday is the first of May.

days.title: Les loisirs
days.objective: Talk about hobbies and free time using jouer à/de and faire du/de la.
days.paretoFocus: The jouer/faire contrast covers most sports and hobbies, a core DELF A1 speaking topic.
drills: Say two sports with 'faire du/de la'
Say two games with 'jouer à'
Say one instrument with 'jouer de'

items: Recall when to use jouer à vs jouer de
Say 'I play football' in French
Say 'I do swimming' in French

activities.title: Les loisirs / Hobbies
activities.intro: Use 'jouer à' for games and sports, 'jouer de' for instruments, and 'faire du/de la' for many activities.

items.hr: J'aime lire et regarder des films.
items.en: I like reading and watching films.

activities.title: Practice: hobbies
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct form for each activity.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I play football'?
questions.explanation: Sports with jouer use 'à': jouer à + le = au.

questions.prompt: Which is correct for 'I play the guitar'?
questions.explanation: Instruments use 'jouer de': de + la = de la.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I go swimming' (faire de la natation).
questions.explanation: faire de la + feminine activity; natation is feminine.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I do sport'.
questions.explanation: faire du + masculine noun: de + le = du.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Je joue de la guitare.'

activities.title: Tes loisirs / Your hobbies
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and talk about your free time.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu fais le week-end ?
lines.en: What do you do on weekends?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je fais du sport et je joue au football.
lines.en: I do sport and I play football.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je joue de la guitare.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'aime lire et regarder des films.
lines.en: I like reading and watching films.

days.title: Au téléphone
days.objective: Learn to answer the phone and make plans to meet someone.
days.paretoFocus: Phone phrases and inviting/accepting are common DELF A1 interaction tasks and reuse time and day vocabulary.
drills: Answer the phone in French
Invite someone to do something
Accept and refuse an invitation politely

items: Recall 'Allô' and 'C'est de la part de qui ?'
Say 'Are you free on Saturday?'
Accept an invitation in French

activities.title: Au téléphone / On the phone
activities.intro: Use these set phrases to answer the phone, invite someone, and arrange a time.

items.note: Allô is only used on the phone

items.hr: C'est de la part de qui ?
items.note: polite way to ask who is calling

items.hr: On se retrouve à quinze heures ?

activities.title: Practice: on the phone
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct phone phrase.

questions.prompt: How do you answer the phone in French?
questions.explanation: Allô is the standard phone greeting.

questions.prompt: Which asks 'Are you free on Saturday?'
Type the French for 'Who's calling?' (phone).
questions.answer: C'est de la part de qui
questions.explanation: C'est de la part de qui ? is the standard polite phrase.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'OK, see you Saturday!'
questions.explanation: à + day means 'see you on (that day)'.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'On se retrouve à quinze heures ?'

activities.title: On se voit samedi ? / Meeting on Saturday
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and arrange a meeting on the phone.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Yes! Are you free on Saturday?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je suis libre. On fait quoi ?
lines.en: Yes, I'm free. What shall we do?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: On va au cinéma ? On se retrouve à quinze heures ?
lines.en: Shall we go to the cinema? Meet at 3 p.m.?

days.title: Les vêtements
days.objective: Learn clothing vocabulary and the verb 'porter' to describe what people wear.
days.paretoFocus: Clothes plus 'porter' let you describe people and shopping situations, common in DELF A1.
drills: Name five clothing items in French
Say what you are wearing today
Use 'porter' in a full sentence

items: Recall five clothing words
Say 'I'm wearing a blue jumper'
Ask 'What are you wearing?'

activities.title: Les vêtements / Clothes
activities.intro: Use 'porter' (to wear) with clothing nouns. Watch the articles un/une.

items.hr: Je porte un pantalon et une chemise.
items.en: I'm wearing trousers and a shirt.

items.hr: Il porte un manteau et des chaussures.
items.en: He's wearing a coat and shoes.

items.hr: Je mets un pull quand il fait froid.
items.en: I put on a jumper when it's cold.

activities.title: Practice: clothes
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct clothing word or form.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'She's wearing a dress'?
questions.explanation: porter = to wear; une robe = a dress (feminine).

questions.prompt: Which word means 'shoes'?
Type the French for 'I'm wearing a shirt' (une chemise).
questions.explanation: porter + une chemise; chemise is feminine.

questions.prompt: Type the French verb meaning 'to wear' (infinitive).
Put in order: 'Il porte un manteau et des chaussures.'

activities.title: Qu'est-ce que tu portes ? / What are you wearing?
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and talk about clothes.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je porte un pantalon et une chemise.
lines.en: I'm wearing trousers and a shirt.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, alors je mets aussi un pull.
lines.en: Yes, so I also put on a jumper.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et pour la fête, tu portes quoi ?
lines.en: And for the party, what are you wearing?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je vais porter une belle veste.
lines.en: I'm going to wear a nice jacket.

days.title: Les couleurs et la description
days.objective: Learn colours and how to describe objects with adjective agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Colours and basic adjective agreement let you describe almost anything, a frequent DELF A1 need.
drills: Name six colours in French
Describe an object with a colour
Make one colour agree in the feminine

items: Recall six colours
Say 'a red car' with agreement
Ask 'What colour is it?'

activities.title: Les couleurs / Colours
activities.intro: Colours usually come after the noun and agree with it. Some are invariable.

items.hr: un pull rouge, une voiture rouge
items.en: a red jumper, a red car

items.hr: un sac vert, une robe verte
items.en: a green bag, a green dress

items.hr: un stylo bleu, une chemise bleue
items.en: a blue pen, a blue shirt

activities.title: Practice: colours and description
activities.intro: Choose or type the correct colour form.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'a green dress'?
questions.explanation: The colour follows the noun and agrees: robe (fem.) → verte.

questions.prompt: Which colour is invariable (same in masc./fem.)?
questions.explanation: rouge already ends in -e, so it doesn't change for the feminine.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'a blue shirt' (une chemise, feminine).
questions.explanation: bleu becomes bleue to agree with the feminine noun chemise.

questions.prompt: Type the French question 'What colour is it?'
questions.explanation: C'est de quelle couleur ? asks about colour.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Le chat est noir et blanc.'

activities.title: De quelle couleur ? / What colour?
activities.intro: Play the role of 'Me' and describe objects and their colours.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: J'aime ton sac ! Il est de quelle couleur ?
lines.en: I like your bag! What colour is it?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et ta voiture, elle est rouge ?
lines.en: And your car, is it red?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai un chat noir et blanc.
lines.en: Yes, I have a black and white cat.

days.title: La maison : décrire son logement et ses pièces
days.objective: Describe your home and name the main rooms.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'il y a' + the core rooms vocabulary, which unlocks most home descriptions.
drills: Name every room in your home aloud in French.
Say three sentences using 'Dans ma maison, il y a...'.
Describe your bedroom in two sentences.

items: Recall the words for 5 rooms without looking.
Say 'there is' and 'there are' in French.
List 3 pieces of furniture from memory.

activities.title: Les pièces de la maison
activities.intro: Learn the main rooms and how to say what a home contains.

items.note: 'il y a' means both 'there is' and 'there are'.

activities.title: Ma maison
activities.intro: Practise rooms and 'il y a'.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'the kitchen'?
How do you say 'there is a bathroom'?
options: il y a une salle de bains
il est une salle de bains
questions.answer: il y a une salle de bains
questions.explanation: 'Il y a' introduces what exists in a place.

questions.prompt: Type 'I live in a house' in French.
questions.explanation: 'habiter' = to live; 'dans une maison' = in a house.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'the bedroom'.
Put in order: 'Il y a un grand salon.'
questions.explanation: The adjective 'grand' comes before the noun 'salon'.

activities.title: Tu habites où ?
activities.intro: Talk about your home. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'habite dans un appartement en ville.
lines.en: I live in an apartment in town.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a combien de pièces ?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il y a trois pièces : un salon, une chambre et une cuisine.
lines.en: There are three rooms: a living room, a bedroom and a kitchen.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Et il y a un jardin ?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, il n'y a pas de jardin, mais il y a un balcon.
lines.en: No, there's no garden, but there is a balcony.

days.title: Dans la ville : les lieux et 'il y a'
days.objective: Name places in town and say what a town has.
days.paretoFocus: Reuse 'il y a' with place vocabulary to describe any town.
drills: List five places in your town in French.
Say 'In my town there is/are...' three times.
Ask 'Is there a...?' about three places.

items: Recall 5 places in town from memory.
Say the negative 'there isn't a...' form.
Name 2 places you go every week.

activities.title: Les lieux de la ville
activities.intro: Learn common places found in a town.

items.hr: Dans ma ville, il y a un cinéma.
items.en: In my town, there is a cinema.

activities.title: Ma ville
activities.intro: Practise places and 'il y a'.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'the train station'?
How do you say 'there isn't a bank'?
options: il n'y a pas de banque
questions.answer: il n'y a pas de banque
questions.explanation: The negative of 'il y a une' is 'il n'y a pas de'.

questions.prompt: Type 'the pharmacy' in French.
Type 'there is a supermarket' in French.
Put in order: 'Il y a une pharmacie ici.'
questions.explanation: 'ici' (here) goes at the end of the sentence.

activities.title: Il y a quoi dans ta ville ?
activities.intro: Describe your town. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Il y a quoi dans ta ville ?
lines.en: What is there in your town?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il y a une gare, un supermarché et un parc.
lines.en: There's a station, a supermarket and a park.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il y a un cinéma dans le centre.
lines.en: Yes, there's a cinema in the centre.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, il n'y a pas de piscine.
lines.en: No, there isn't a swimming pool.

days.title: Demander son chemin : demander et indiquer la direction
days.objective: Ask for and give simple directions.
days.paretoFocus: Learn 'où est...?' plus a few direction verbs to navigate any request.
drills: Ask where three places are using 'Où est...?'.
Give directions using 'tournez' and 'tout droit'.
Practise 'à gauche' and 'à droite' with gestures.

items: Recall 'left', 'right', 'straight ahead'.
Say 'Excuse me, where is the station?'.
Recall 'turn' and 'go' in the polite form.

activities.title: Indiquer le chemin
activities.intro: Learn words to ask for and give directions.

activities.title: Les directions
activities.intro: Practise asking and giving directions.

questions.prompt: What does 'tout droit' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Tout droit' means straight ahead; don't confuse it with 'à droite' (right).

questions.prompt: How do you politely ask 'Where is the pharmacy?'
Type 'turn left' in French (polite form).
Type 'to the right' in French.
Put in order: 'Tournez à droite ici.'
questions.explanation: Verb first (command), then direction, then place.

activities.title: Pardon, où est la gare ?
activities.intro: You are lost and ask for directions. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Excuse me, where is the station?

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Allez tout droit, puis tournez à gauche.
lines.en: Go straight ahead, then turn left.

days.title: Les transports : se déplacer en ville
days.objective: Talk about how you get around using different transports.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'prendre' and 'en/à' with transport words to describe any journey.
drills: Say how you go to work or school in French.
Use 'Je prends le bus/le métro/le train' three times.
Practise 'à pied' and 'à vélo'.

items: Recall 5 modes of transport from memory.
Conjugate 'prendre' in 'je' and 'tu'.
Say 'I go by bus' and 'I go on foot'.

activities.title: Les moyens de transport
activities.intro: Learn transport words and how to say you take them.

activities.title: Se déplacer
activities.intro: Practise transports and 'prendre'.

questions.prompt: Which sentence means 'I take the metro'?
How do you say 'on foot'?
Type 'I take the train' in French.
Type 'by bike' in French.
Put in order: 'Je prends le bus le matin.'
questions.explanation: Subject + verb + object, then the time expression 'le matin'.

activities.title: Tu prends quoi pour aller au travail ?
activities.intro: Talk about your commute. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Tu prends quoi pour aller au travail ?
lines.en: What do you take to go to work?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Je prends le métro tous les jours.
lines.en: I take the metro every day.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Le week-end, je vais à vélo ou à pied.
lines.en: At the weekend, I go by bike or on foot.

days.title: Faire les courses : les quantités
days.objective: Buy food and express quantities at the market.
days.paretoFocus: Learn 'un kilo de / une bouteille de' patterns to express any quantity.
drills: Ask for three foods using 'Je voudrais...'.
Say quantities: 'un kilo de', 'un litre de', 'une bouteille de'.
Practise 'C'est combien ?' with three items.

items: Recall 5 foods from memory.
Say 'a kilo of apples' and 'a litre of milk'.
Ask 'How much is it?' in French.

activities.title: Les courses et les quantités
activities.intro: Learn food words and quantity expressions with 'de'.

activities.title: Au marché
activities.intro: Practise quantities and food shopping.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'a kilo of apples'?
questions.explanation: Quantities use 'de' + noun without an article.

questions.prompt: Which means 'a bottle of water'?
Type 'I would like some bread' in French.
Type 'How much is it?' in French.
Put in order: 'Je voudrais un litre de lait.'
questions.explanation: 'un litre de lait' keeps 'de' before the noun.

activities.title: Chez le marchand
activities.intro: You are buying fruit at the market. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais un kilo de pommes.
lines.en: Hello, I'd like a kilo of apples.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Une bouteille d'eau, s'il vous plaît.

days.title: Au magasin : les vêtements, les tailles et les prix
days.objective: Shop for clothes and talk about sizes and prices.
days.paretoFocus: Combine clothes vocabulary with 'Je cherche' and size/price questions.
drills: Name five clothing items in French.
Say 'I'm looking for a...' with three items.
Ask for your size using 'Je fais du...'.

items: Recall 5 clothing words from memory.
Ask 'Do you have this in my size?'.
Say a price like 'trente euros'.

activities.title: Les vêtements
activities.intro: Learn clothing words plus size and price phrases.

items.note: 'faire du' is used for clothing/shoe sizes.

activities.title: Faire du shopping
activities.intro: Practise clothes, sizes and prices.

questions.prompt: Which word means 'a dress'?
How do you say 'I'm a size 38'?
questions.explanation: Use 'faire du' to state a clothing size.

questions.prompt: Type 'I'm looking for shoes' in French.
Type 'How much does it cost?' in French.
Put in order: 'Je cherche un pantalon noir.'
questions.explanation: The colour adjective 'noir' comes after the noun.

activities.title: Dans une boutique
activities.intro: You are shopping for clothes. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, je cherche une robe bleue.
lines.en: Yes, I'm looking for a blue dress.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Here you are. It costs forty euros.

days.title: L'argent et payer : régler ses achats
days.objective: Handle money and pay for purchases.
days.paretoFocus: Learn payment phrases and price questions to complete any transaction.
drills: Ask the total using 'Ça fait combien ?'.
Say 'I'll pay by card' and 'in cash'.
Practise saying prices in euros aloud.

items: Recall 'card', 'cash', 'change' in French.
Say 'Can I pay by card?'.
Read three prices aloud in euros.

activities.title: Payer et l'argent
activities.intro: Learn how to ask the price and pay.

items.note: 'la monnaie' is change/coins, not 'money' in general.

activities.title: À la caisse
activities.intro: Practise paying and money words.

questions.prompt: How do you ask 'How much is that in total?'
questions.explanation: 'Ça fait combien ?' asks for the total.

questions.prompt: Which means 'in cash'?
Type 'Can I pay by card?' in French.
Type the French word for 'the change' (coins returned).
questions.explanation: 'La monnaie' is the change you get back.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'Je peux payer en espèces ?'

activities.title: Payer à la caisse
activities.intro: You are paying at the checkout. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Here's your receipt. Have a good day!

days.title: Réserver : un hôtel ou une table avec 'je voudrais'
days.objective: Make a hotel or restaurant reservation politely.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'Je voudrais' + details to make any polite booking.
drills: Book a room using 'Je voudrais réserver une chambre'.
Book a table for two using 'Je voudrais une table pour deux'.
State dates with 'du ... au ...'.

items: Recall 'Je voudrais réserver...'.
Say 'a table for two people'.
Ask 'Do you have a room available?'.

activities.title: Faire une réservation
activities.intro: Learn polite phrases to book a room or a table.

items.en: I'd like to book a room.
items.note: 'Je voudrais' is the polite form of 'je veux'.

items.en: Do you have a room available?

activities.title: Réserver
activities.intro: Practise booking a room or a table.

questions.prompt: How do you politely say 'I'd like to book a room'?
questions.explanation: 'Je voudrais' + infinitive is the polite booking form.

questions.prompt: Which means 'a table for two people'?
Type 'Do you have a room available?' in French.
Type 'for two nights' in French.
Put in order: 'Je voudrais une table pour deux.'

activities.title: À la réception de l'hôtel
activities.intro: You are booking a hotel room by phone. You play 'Me'.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour, je voudrais réserver une chambre.
lines.en: Hello, I'd like to book a room.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Pour deux nuits, s'il vous plaît.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Under the name Martin. Thank you!

days.title: Décrire une personne
days.objective: Describe someone's physical appearance using adjectives with correct gender and number agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Master 'il/elle est' + adjective and place common adjectives with proper agreement.
drills: Describe three people around you using 'Il/Elle est...' with two adjectives each.
Turn five masculine adjectives into their feminine forms out loud.
Write two sentences using 'avoir les cheveux/yeux' + colour.

items: Recall the feminine forms of grand, petit, beau, brun.
Say your own physical description in three sentences.
List five colours in French.

activities.title: Adjectifs de description
activities.intro: Key adjectives to describe physical appearance, shown masculine then feminine.

items.hr: Il est grand, elle est grande.
items.en: He is tall, she is tall.

items.hr: Il est petit, elle est petite.
items.en: He is short, she is short.

items.hr: Elle a les cheveux longs et bruns.
Elle est belle, il est beau.
items.en: She is beautiful, he is handsome.

activities.title: Accord des adjectifs
activities.intro: Practice agreement and vocabulary of physical description.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'Marie is tall.'
questions.explanation: Marie is feminine singular, so 'grand' becomes 'grande'.

questions.prompt: Which is the feminine of 'beau'?
Complete: 'Elle a les cheveux ___ (long, feminine plural agreement).'
questions.explanation: 'Cheveux' is masculine plural, so the adjective is 'longs'.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'He has blue eyes.'
Put in order: 'Elle est petite et brune.'
questions.explanation: Subject + être + adjectives, all feminine to agree with 'elle'.

activities.title: Tu es comment ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks you to describe a classmate.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Ton ami Paul, il est comment ?
lines.en: Your friend Paul, what does he look like?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il est grand et il a les cheveux bruns.
lines.en: He is tall and he has brown hair.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il est beau et sympa.
lines.en: Yes, he is handsome and nice.

days.title: Le caractère
days.objective: Describe someone's personality using common character adjectives with agreement.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'être' + personality adjectives and connect them with 'mais' and 'et'.
drills: Describe two family members' personalities with two adjectives each.
Make five sentences contrasting traits using 'mais'.
Convert five personality adjectives to the feminine.

items: Recall the feminine of sérieux, gentil, timide, sportif.
Say three adjectives that describe you.
Give the opposite of 'sympa' and 'calme'.

activities.title: Adjectifs de caractère
activities.intro: Common personality adjectives with feminine forms.

items.hr: Il est gentil, elle est gentille.
items.en: He is kind, she is kind.

items.hr: Il est sérieux, elle est sérieuse.
items.en: He is serious, she is serious.

items.hr: Il est sympa mais un peu paresseux.
items.en: He is nice but a bit lazy.

activities.title: Décrire le caractère
activities.intro: Practice personality adjectives and their agreement.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct sentence: 'She is kind.'
Which word means 'shy'?
Complete: 'Marie est ___ (serious, feminine).'
Type the French for 'He is nice but lazy.'
Put in order: 'Elle est intelligente et gentille.'
questions.explanation: Both adjectives take the feminine form to agree with 'elle'.

activities.title: Comment est ta sœur ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about your sister's personality.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, elle n'est pas timide, elle est sociable.
lines.en: No, she is not shy, she is sociable.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, elle est sérieuse et travailleuse.
lines.en: Yes, she is serious and hardworking.

days.title: Le temps qu'il fait
days.objective: Talk about the weather using impersonal expressions with 'il fait' and 'il y a'.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'Il fait...', 'Il y a...', and 'Il pleut/neige' to describe weather.
drills: Describe today's weather in three sentences.
Match five weather expressions to their English meaning.
Ask and answer 'Quel temps fait-il ?' for four cities.

items: Recall the weather verbs pleuvoir and neiger in 'il' form.
Say four things using 'Il fait...'.
Give the question 'What's the weather like?' in French.

activities.title: La météo
activities.intro: Impersonal weather expressions used with 'il'.

activities.title: Quel temps fait-il ?
activities.intro: Practice weather expressions.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'It is raining'?
Which expression means 'It is windy'?
questions.explanation: Wind uses 'il y a du vent'.

questions.prompt: Complete: 'En hiver, il ___ froid.'
Type the French for 'It is snowing.'
Put in order: 'Aujourd'hui il fait beau.'

activities.title: La météo aujourd'hui
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about the weather where you are.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: What's the weather like where you are?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Il fait froid et il pleut.
lines.en: It is cold and it is raining.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, il y a beaucoup de vent.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Tomorrow, the weather is going to be nice.

days.title: Les saisons
days.objective: Name the four seasons and say what activities you do in each.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'en été/automne/hiver' and 'au printemps' plus common seasonal activities.
drills: Say one activity you do in each season.
Match each season to typical weather.
Write three sentences with 'en été je...' style.

items: Recall the four seasons in order.
Remember which season uses 'au' (printemps).
Say two summer and two winter activities.

activities.title: Les quatre saisons
activities.intro: The seasons and how to say 'in' each one.

items.hr: En été, je vais à la plage.
items.en: In summer, I go to the beach.

activities.title: Saisons et activités
activities.intro: Practice seasons and seasonal activities.

questions.prompt: Which is correct for 'in spring'?
questions.explanation: Spring is the exception and uses 'au printemps'.

questions.prompt: Which season is 'l'hiver'?
Complete: '___ été, il fait chaud.'
Type the French for 'In winter, I ski.'
questions.answer: En hiver, je fais du ski.

questions.prompt: Put in order: 'En été je vais à la plage.'

activities.title: Ta saison préférée
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend asks about your favourite season.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En été, il fait beau et je vais à la plage.
lines.en: In summer, the weather is nice and I go to the beach.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: En hiver, je fais du ski avec ma famille.
lines.en: In winter, I ski with my family.

days.title: Le corps
days.objective: Name the main parts of the body with correct articles.
days.paretoFocus: Learn body-part nouns with 'le/la/les' and the structure 'j'ai...'.
drills: Point to five body parts and name them in French.
Sort ten body parts by gender (le/la).
Say 'J'ai deux...' for parts that come in pairs.

items: Recall the articles for tête, main, bras, jambe.
Name the five senses' organs (eyes, ears, nose...).
List body parts from head to foot.

activities.title: Les parties du corps
activities.intro: Main body parts with their articles.

activities.title: Nommer le corps
activities.intro: Practice body-part vocabulary and articles.

questions.prompt: What is 'the head' in French?
Which word means 'the hand'?
Complete with the article: '___ bras' (the arm).
Type the French for 'the feet' (plural).
Put in order: 'J'ai mal à la tête.'
questions.explanation: 'avoir mal à' + body part expresses pain.

activities.title: Le corps en cours
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A teacher does a body-parts warm-up with you.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.en: Here it is, this is the head.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: How many legs do you have?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: J'ai deux jambes et deux pieds.
lines.en: I have two legs and two feet.

days.title: Chez le médecin
days.objective: Explain what hurts at the doctor's using 'avoir mal à'.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'avoir mal à' with contractions (au, à la, à l', aux) plus basic doctor phrases.
drills: Say five sentences with 'J'ai mal à/au/aux...'.
Practice the contractions of 'à + le/la/les' with body parts.
Role-play describing three symptoms.

items: Recall 'à + le = au' and 'à + les = aux'.
Say 'I have a headache' and 'I have a stomachache'.
Remember the phrase 'Où avez-vous mal ?'.

activities.title: Avoir mal à
activities.intro: Expressing pain with the correct contraction of 'à'.

activities.title: Où avez-vous mal ?
activities.intro: Practice pain expressions and contractions.

questions.prompt: Choose the correct form: 'I have a stomachache.'
Which is correct: 'I have a toothache'?
Complete: 'J'ai mal ___ gorge.' (throat)
questions.explanation: 'Gorge' is feminine, so 'à la gorge' with no contraction.

questions.prompt: Type the French for 'I am sick.'
Put in order: 'J'ai mal au ventre.'

activities.title: Une visite chez le médecin
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. You visit the doctor because you feel unwell.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Bonjour docteur, j'ai mal à la tête.
lines.en: Hello doctor, I have a headache.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, et j'ai mal à la gorge.
lines.en: Yes, and I have a sore throat.

days.title: Les émotions
days.objective: Express feelings and physical states using 'avoir' expressions.
days.paretoFocus: Use 'avoir peur/faim/froid/chaud/soif' and 'être content/triste'.
drills: Say five states with 'J'ai...' (faim, soif, froid...).
Contrast 'avoir' expressions with 'être content/triste'.
Describe how you feel in three situations.

items: Recall which feelings use 'avoir' vs 'être'.
Say 'I am hungry', 'I am cold', 'I am afraid'.
Give the feminine of 'content' and 'heureux'.

activities.title: Avoir et les émotions
activities.intro: Feelings and states, many using 'avoir' where English uses 'to be'.

items.hr: Je suis content, je suis contente.

activities.title: Exprimer ses émotions
activities.intro: Practice avoir/être feeling expressions.

questions.prompt: How do you say 'I am hungry'?
Which uses 'être' correctly?
Complete: 'J'ai ___.' (I am afraid)
Type the French for 'She is happy.' (feminine)
Put in order: 'J'ai froid et faim.'
questions.explanation: Both states share one 'avoir', linked by 'et'.

activities.title: Comment tu te sens ?
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. A friend checks how you are feeling.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.en: Are you okay? Are you hungry?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Oui, j'ai faim et j'ai soif.
lines.en: Yes, I am hungry and thirsty.

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Non, mais je suis un peu fatigué.
lines.en: No, but I am a bit tired.

days.title: Bilan A1
days.objective: Review core A1 skills: greetings, présent, passé composé, and futur proche.
days.paretoFocus: Recognize when to use présent, passé composé, and futur proche in simple sentences.
drills: Give one sentence each in présent, passé composé, and futur proche.
Greet and introduce yourself in four sentences.
Conjugate 'aller' and 'avoir' in the present out loud.

items: Recall the passé composé formula (avoir/être + participe passé).
Recall the futur proche formula (aller + infinitif).
Say a full self-introduction from memory.

activities.title: Les trois temps de base
activities.intro: A quick recap of the three A1 tenses and greetings.

items.en: I am going to eat an apple.

activities.title: Réviser les temps
activities.intro: Mixed review of the three tenses and greetings.

questions.prompt: Which sentence is in the futur proche?
Which auxiliary does 'aller' use in the passé composé?
Complete the passé composé: 'J'___ mangé.' (auxiliary)
Type the French for 'I am going to travel.' (futur proche)
Put in order: 'Je suis allé au cinéma.'
questions.explanation: Passé composé of 'aller' with 'être' + past participle 'allé'.

activities.title: Bilan : parler de soi
activities.intro: You play 'Me'. You review greetings and the three tenses with a partner.

lines.speaker: Partner
lines.hr: Qu'est-ce que tu as fait hier ?

lines.speaker: Me
lines.hr: Demain, je vais aller à la plage.
lines.en: Tomorrow, I am going to go to the beach.
